commit.message:
Migrate board event identity to author/session semantics

Move board event identity from the legacy userId-only model to
explicit authorUserId + sessionId semantics across the microboard
library surface.

This updates local event creation to emit authorUserId and sessionId,
keeps userId only as a compatibility mirror/fallback, and migrates
self-echo detection plus undo/redo ownership to session-scoped logic.
Batch expansion and replay now preserve the new identity fields, while
legacy records that only carry body.userId continue to resolve as old
session-like origins during the migration.

Presence handling is also moved to session-scoped identity. Remote
presence actors are keyed by session, no longer collapsed by shared
account identity, and transport-facing presence message types now carry
sessionId and authorUserId metadata.

Tests cover same-author different-session remote behavior, legacy
body.userId fallback, outbound batching metadata, and session-scoped
undo ownership. Build and focused test runs pass after the migration.

commit.message:
fix: restore GPU CI job (#23)

## Summary
- keep the GPU CI on the archived two-stage flow while removing the
stale bellman-cuda / CRS setup
- fix archived integration tests to resolve `test_fixtures` and
`artifacts/app.bin` from the runtime `CARGO_MANIFEST_DIR` that nextest
remaps on the GPU runner
- remove the stale `WORKSPACE_DIR` override from the archive build step
because it was not actually fixing archive replay

## Validation
- `cargo fmt -p ethereum_prover -- --check`
- `ZKSYNC_USE_CUDA_STUBS=true cargo nextest list -p ethereum_prover
--test integration --release`
- `ZKSYNC_USE_CUDA_STUBS=true cargo nextest archive --release -p
ethereum_prover --test integration --archive-file
prover-gpu-tests-local.tar.zst`
- `cargo nextest run --archive-file prover-gpu-tests-local.tar.zst
--workspace-remap "$(pwd)" cpu_witness_from_fixture_block`

## Context
The GPU CI failure was not missing fixture files in the workflow
checkout. The archived test binary was using compile-time
`env!("CARGO_MANIFEST_DIR")`, so after being built in the container it
still pointed at the container path when replayed on the GPU runner.
Switching the test helper to the runtime `CARGO_MANIFEST_DIR` makes the
archive replay pick up the remapped GPU-runner checkout path as
intended.

feat(orchestrator): add scheduler engine and recovery core

Summary:
- add a generic orchestrator scheduler engine with bounded dispatch,
  worker tracking, retry handling, stall detection, and manifest-backed
  recovery
- extend orchestrator integration tests with fake tracker, workspace,
  and worker backends that cover continuation retry, backoff,
  terminal cleanup, and recovery
- document the shipped scheduler contract across architecture,
  runtime, Linear, workspace, and operations docs

Rationale:
- the repository had explicit domain transitions and dispatch helpers,
  but no scheduler-owned loop that could drive real claim/run/retry/
  release behavior
- keeping the scheduler generic over tracker, workspace, and worker
  backends makes orchestration policy testable now while preserving a
  thin follow-on seam for the concrete OpenHands worker adapter

Tests:

feat: add idle detection and enhanced approval patterns for CLI agents (#521)

* refactor(cli-agent): prefer submitted commands over raw chunks

Summary:
- add a CLI agent input accumulator and route terminal input through
  submitted commands instead of per-keystroke detection
- collapse buffered output handling into a single service entrypoint
  that runs fallback startup, termination, and waiting in order
- add regression tests for submit-time connection and same-flush
  waiting detection

Rationale:
- keystroke-level detection could never recognize commands like
  "gemini" typed one character at a time
- waiting detection was split across separate calls, which made the
  connected-to-waiting transition fragile inside a single flush
- centralizing the pipeline keeps the existing detectors but makes the
  runtime flow easier to reason about and test

- npx vitest run src/test/vitest/unit/terminals/TerminalIOCoordinator.test.ts src/test/vitest/unit/services/CliAgentDetectionService.test.ts src/test/vitest/unit/terminals/TerminalDataBufferManager.test.ts

commit.message:
fix(seo): remove [slug]/loading.tsx that hides H1 from crawlers (#830)

The loading.tsx at the [slug] level created an automatic Suspense
boundary around ALL storefront sub-pages. During SSR, Next.js rendered
StorefrontPageSkeleton as the initial HTML (no H1), with actual page
content only in RSC streaming payload scripts. Crawlers that don't
execute JavaScript (Ahrefs, some Googlebot modes) only saw the
skeleton — zero H1 tags.

The home page already has its own <Suspense> wrapper with the skeleton
fallback, so this file was redundant there and harmful for SEO on all
other pages (about, privacy, faq, contact, terms, etc.).

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>
Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix(web): honor DAO RPC ordering in wagmi config (#560)

Summary:
- normalize DAO chain metadata in a shared helper reused by the dapp
  provider
- build explicit viem transports from DAO-configured RPC URLs, keep
  their fallback order stable, and fail fast when none are configured
- avoid adding the built-in mainnet chain when the DAO itself already
  runs on chain id 1

Rationale:
- duplicate mainnet entries let RainbowKit and wagmi prefer built-in
  Ethereum RPC defaults over daoConfig.chain.rpcs
- explicit fallback transports preserve DAO RPC priority and fail over
  to the next configured endpoint when one RPC is unavailable
- empty RPC lists should fail with a clear DAO-chain error instead of
  deferring to an unusable transport with no URL

Tests:
- pnpm exec eslint src/config/wagmi.ts src/providers/dapp.provider.tsx
  src/components/members-table/hooks/useMembersData.ts
- pnpm exec tsc --noEmit
- pnpm dlx tsx -e ordered fallback transport probe
- pnpm dlx tsx -e empty RPC error probe
- curl -fsSL https://api.degov.ai/dao/config/fluence-dao?format=yml

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
fix: handle transient L1 RPC failures (#22)

## Summary
- add explicit retry helpers around L1 head and block-input RPC reads
- keep the continuous block stream alive on repeated L1 failures and
skip the block after retries instead of bubbling the error up and
shutting down the runtime
- reuse the same retry wrapper for single-block RPC fetches and make
EthProofs upload logs only report success on actual success
- checked the local Alloy 1.4.3 retry layer sources; the built-in policy
is centered on retryable transport/rate-limit responses, so
connection-timeout handling stays explicit at the block-stream boundary

## Testing
- `ZKSYNC_USE_CUDA_STUBS=1 cargo check -p ethereum_prover --lib`
- `RUST_MIN_STACK=16777216 ZKSYNC_USE_CUDA_STUBS=1 cargo test -p
ethereum_prover --lib -- --nocapture`
- `RUST_MIN_STACK=16777216 ZKSYNC_USE_CUDA_STUBS=1 cargo nextest run -p
ethereum_prover --lib`

commit.message:
fix(web): move LLM markdown routes to /api/llm/ to fix storefront routing (#827)

* fix(web): move LLM markdown routes to /api/llm/ to fix storefront routing collision

The `.md` directory routes (e.g., `index.html.md/route.ts`) inside the
storefront `[slug]/` route tree caused a Next.js App Router collision
with the dynamic `[category]` segment. On custom domains like
ogabassey.com, the homepage resolved to a category page titled
"Index.Html.Md" instead of the merchant storefront.

Fix: Consolidate all 8 per-page `.md` route handlers into a single
catch-all API route at `/api/llm/[...segments]/route.ts`. The proxy
now intercepts `.md` requests in three contexts (custom domains,
subdomains, and slug-based paths) and rewrites them to the API handler.

The external URLs remain spec-compliant (e.g., /index.html.md still
returns markdown), but the routes no longer live in the storefront
tree where they can collide with dynamic segments.

Introduced in: c9cb732d6 (PR #785)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* test: add colocated tests for consolidated /api/llm route (#827)

commit.message:
fix: restore multicall batching for DAO RPC reads (#559)

* fix(web): restore multicall batching for RPC reads

Summary:
- fix the shared multicall batch size to use viem's byte-based default
- configure wagmi transports from each chain's DAO RPC list via fallback
- make shared wagmi multicall batching explicit for public read traffic

Rationale:
- the previous batch size of 10 bytes split existing multicall reads into
  near-single-call requests
- RainbowKit's default transport setup only created one http transport per
  chain, which skipped the DAO RPC fallback list from config
- reducing fan-out at the shared client layer lowers pressure on public RPCs
  without rewriting each page-level hook

Tests:
- cd packages/web && pnpm lint
- cd packages/web && pnpm exec tsc --noEmit

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>
(cherry picked from commit cb724fb8759aa7ef9421e4e117be0313dfc6ee8c)

* fix(web): harden wagmi transport selection

Summary:
- preserve DAO RPC ordering by disabling viem fallback ranking
- dedupe configured chains and transports by chain id before creating wagmi
  config
- fail fast when a chain has no configured RPC URLs

Rationale:
- automated review surfaced edge cases that could silently reorder RPC
  selection or duplicate mainnet entries
- raising an explicit config error is easier to diagnose than a later
  request-time transport failure

Tests:
- cd packages/web && pnpm lint
